title: Frankenstein Identity Synthetic - Real SSN with Fabricated PII Layering
narrative: A fraudster combines a legitimate SSN (often from a child or elderly victim) with entirely fabricated name, address, and contact information to create a synthetic identity. The account builds credit over 4-6 months with small authorized user tradelines ($300-$800 limits) before attempting a bust-out. Transaction pattern shows initial organic behavior: $12-$45 purchases at grocery/gas MCCs, then sudden shift to high-value electronics ($1,200-$2,800) and gift card loads ($500 exact amounts). The R_emaildomain switches from a 6-month-old Gmail to a newly created ProtonMail 48 hours before bust-out. Card velocity jumps from 2-3 monthly transactions to 8+ in 72 hours. The dist1 (distance between billing/shipping) explodes from consistent 0-5km to 850+ km as goods ship to freight forwarders. addr1 shows no prior transaction history across the network despite 6+ month account age.
indicators: card1_txn_count_7d increases from 0-1 baseline to 8+ transactions within 72 hours
TransactionAmt pattern: 90+ days of $12-$85 purchases, then sudden $1,200-$2,800 electronics transactions
R_emaildomain age <14 days while account age shows 180+ days in bureau data
dist1 metric shifts from consistent <5km to 800+ km in final transaction cluster
addr1 shows zero historical footprint in transaction network despite 6-month seasoning period
Gift card ProductCD transactions in exact $500 increments clustered within 6-hour window
card1_amt_zscore jumps from -0.3 baseline to +3.8 during bust-out phase
summary: A real Social Security Number is paired with completely fake personal details—name, address, email—to build a credit profile over 4-6 months with small purchases, then suddenly shifts to large electronics buys and gift card loads while the email changes and shipping distances spike from local to 850+ km.
reasoning: The fraudster uses a stolen SSN (often from a child or elderly victim) to pass identity checks while controlling all contact points, building trust with small transactions before cashing out through high-value goods shipped to freight forwarders, indicating premeditated theft rather than legitimate spending.
indicator_explanations: Card goes from dormant to hyperactive with sudden transaction surge
Small purchases establish trust before massive electronics fraud hit
Email address is brand new despite account claiming months of history
Final transactions appear hundreds of miles from established location pattern
Address has no transaction history despite supposedly existing for months
Exact dollar gift card purchases clustered tightly suggest monetization phase
Spending amount explodes to extreme deviation after establishing normal baseline
